tracks.meaning: a solid piece of material such as stone, brick, or wood, typically with flat surfaces
a large amount of computer memory or data allocated as a unit
to obstruct or prevent the movement or progress of something
the area of a city or country, typically one that is densely populated or characterized by poverty
in various sports, a player's position or a formation of players in a line
an emotional or mental barrier that prevents communication or understanding
a large or significant quantity of something
in sports, to illegally obstruct or impede an opposing player
to prevent someone from pursuing a legal action or claim through legal procedure
a city area containing a group of houses or buildings, or a neighborhood subdivision
a large quantity of shares or securities held or traded together
a set of scenes or acts presented as a unified theatrical unit
a failure or stoppage in the flow of communication or conversation
